testnet.notes: FRESH REPUBLISH 2026-06-04 (digest FmFja419…). mock_lender rebuilt as a real cToken/share money market mirroring Suilend/Scallop: suppliers receive SHARES priced by a global compounding `exchange_rate` (1e12 scale); `reserve_factor_bps` (1000 = 10%) routes the protocol's cut of accrued interest into a `reserves` balance claimable to the treasury via `claim_reserves` (on-chain yield spread). `supply`/`supply_into`/`redeem` mint/burn shares (round-down); `redeem` clamps to `backing` and never aborts; `accrue_interest` compounds the index per interaction; `set_apy` is forward-only. The one testnet concession: supplier interest is funded from a pre-seeded `backing` balance (admin `fund`) standing in for borrower repayments. spending_vault: deposit adds shares into the same Position (supply_into); withdraw redeems→splits→re-supplies remainder; public signatures unchanged so the app needs only id/config updates. NOTE: fresh publish = new type ids, so prior Save positions + Merchant registrations from 0x2c778e1d… / 0x29d03305… are orphaned (re-register/re-deposit lazily). Backing still needs seeding with testnet USDC (mock_lender::fund) before yield pays out. UPGRADED 2026-06-04 (digest H7vUrqTh…, recordPaymentPackageId 0x3ac880a1…) to add payment_receipt::record_payment<T>(&Merchant, amount, memo, invoice_id, clock) — the best-effort receipt leg of the two-leg payment (mints Receipt + PaymentMade, no coin moved). Types + existing entry fns stay at packageId (type origin preserved); record_payment is called at recordPaymentPackageId (constant briskRecordPkg / backend BRISK_RECORD_PKG). Supersedes 0x2c778e1d… / upgrade 0x29d03305… UPGRADED 2026-06-09 (digest 66QqQTR1…, giftCardPackageId 0xaf1ea7a0…) to add the gift_card module — on-chain escrowed, claimable (blake2b256 hashed-secret) closed-loop gift cards. New types + entry fns (mint/claim/redeem/create_config/set_fee/set_treasury) live at giftCardPackageId. create_config was called once post-upgrade (Sui doesn't run module init for upgrade-added modules on testnet 126) → shared GiftCardConfig 0xa50c8948… (fee_bps 300, treasury = deployer 0x076a67…) + GiftCardAdminCap 0x302bd28b… (held by deployer). Add giftCardPackageId's gift_card::{mint,claim,redeem} to BOTH the backend serverAllowedTargets AND the Enoki dashboard allowlist. UPGRADED 2026-06-09 (digest FUMaTs9U…, giftCardPackageId 0x16fa2f6c…, version 5) — REWORKED gift_card to the MERCHANT-PREPAID PROMISE model: mint now pays the merchant their net (face - fee) AND the treasury its fee IMMEDIATELY at issuance, and the GiftCard holds NO escrow (the frozen `balance` field is always empty); `redeem` moves no funds and just draws down the remaining promise, tracked in the `face_value` field (decremented per redeem). Struct + public signatures + event shapes were kept byte-identical so the upgrade passed compatibility; only mint/redeem/balance_of bodies changed. GiftCardConfig (0xa50c8948…) + AdminCap (0x302bd28b…) reused unchanged (fee_bps 300, treasury = deployer 0x076a67…). The new package id must replace the old one in BOTH the backend serverAllowedTargets AND the Enoki dashboard allowlist (targets are gift_card::{mint,claim,redeem} at the v5 id). NOTE: any v4-era escrowed test cards keep their old escrowed funds (now unreachable by v5 redeem) — testnet throwaway, ignore. UPGRADED 2026-06-09 (digest FoV7wi9K…, giftCardPackageId 0xc90ebfad…, version 6) — added gift_card::regift<T>(card, new_claim_hash) (+ GiftCardRegifted event): the current recipient resets the card (recipient → none, new claim_hash) so they can re-gift a received card onward with a fresh secret link. Pure additions (new public fun + new event struct), so the upgrade stayed compatible. The v6 package id must replace v5 in BOTH the backend serverAllowedTargets AND the Enoki dashboard allowlist, and the allowlists must now ALSO include gift_card::regift (targets: gift_card::{mint,claim,redeem,regift} at the v6 id).
